description:
Choropleth map displaying a quantitative distribution across administrative regions in Europe, including EU Member States and neighbouring countries. Country borders are outlined in red. The colour scale uses shades of green to represent values, with an additional "No Data" category.
The legend defines the classes:
*   Pink: No Data
*   Lightest green: 0 - 1.3
*   Light green: 1.3 - 2.5
*   Medium green: 2.5 - 3.8
*   Dark green: 3.8 - 5.5
*   Darkest green: 5.5 - 9.5

Higher values (darkest green, 5.5 - 9.5) are observed in western Ireland, parts of western France, coastal regions of Spain and Portugal, and southern Italy (e.g., Sicily). Other areas with relatively high values (3.8 - 5.5) include parts of the United Kingdom, other areas of France, and specific regions in Greece. Lower values (lightest green, 0 - 1.3) are prevalent across Scandinavia (Sweden, Finland, Norway), most of Eastern Europe, and large parts of Central Europe, including Germany, Austria, and Czechia. One region in northern Ireland is marked as "No Data". No specific unit or reference year is provided in the image.
